channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: You don’t have to spend more than $50 on a great USB-C dock for your Switch 2
item.description: Nintendo seemingly designed its latest console to be a mystery for third-party accessory makers. With the Switch 2, the company changed the wireless protocol for connecting controllers to the new...

item.title: I Couldn’t Afford Earth, So I Built Something Better
item.description: This is a submission for the DEV April Fools Challenge The day has finally come. I am launching my dream project. For months I had been thinking about what to pitch and now I finally am pitching...

item.title: Do Not Press -- an extremely important button that you should not press
item.description: This is a submission for the DEV April Fools Challenge What I Built I built a button. One button. A big, red, very important button that says DO NOT PRESS. It does nothing. There is no easter egg....

item.title: Shipping Features with Claude Code in 2026
item.description: An updated, battle-tested workflow for using Claude Code to ship four features in ~30 minutes refreshed for Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, plugins, skills, and the new GitHub Actions integration I use...

item.title: Your agent can DROP TABLE, read /etc/passwd, and drain a wallet. By default, nothing stops it.
item.description: AI agents are incredible. They write code, query databases, call APIs, manage infrastructure, and now — thanks to protocols like x402 and AP2 — they can spend money autonomously. But here's the...

item.title: The System Went Down
item.description: The system went down. One leader opened Slack. The other opened the logs. Same title. Same years of experience. Same team size. The difference was proximity to the system they were responsible...

item.title: I Built a Side-by-Side Base64 / URL / HTML / Encoder and Finally Stopped Confusing Them
item.description: I Built a Side-by-Side Base64 / URL / HTML / Encoder and Finally Stopped Confusing Them There are only four text encoding schemes you run into daily on the web. And yet the moment you feed them...

item.title: How to breathe in fewer microplastics in your home
item.description: Article URL: https://www.bbc.com/future/article/20260410-how-to-breathe-in-fewer-microplastics-in-your-home Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47730196 Points: 4 # Comments: 0

item.title: Building a Multimodal Cross Cloud Live Agent with ADK, Amazon EKS, and Gemini CLI
item.description: Leveraging the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) and the underlying Gemini LLM to build cross cloud apps with the Python programming language deployed to the EKS container service on AWS. Aren’t...

item.title: Building a Linux Kernel Module in Rust: Zero Panics in 14 Months Production
item.description: How Rust’s type system prevented 23 memory safety bugs that crashed our C kernel module weekly Building a Linux Kernel Module in Rust: Zero Panics in 14 Months Production How Rust’s type system...

item.title: Demons and pinball are a perfect match
item.description: There's one very specific reason I keep a Wii U handy, and that's so that I have an easy way to play the classic pinball game Devil's Crush. Over the years, it has become a comfort game for me....

item.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
item.description: In the early days of the war on Iran, while the White House was busy posting Call of Duty memes and AI slop of dancing bowling pins, the Iranian regime's state media was flooding the zone with...

item.title: The Meeting Skill No Coding Bootcamp Teaches You
item.description: For the first three years of my career, I was invisible in meetings. I'd sit there, listen, nod, and leave. Sometimes I had an idea but didn't say it — I figured someone more experienced would say...

item.title: The new show making fun of tech bros
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 123, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, which Artemis photo did you make your wallpaper, and also you can...

item.title: Is the ‘Holy Grail of batteries’ finally ready to bless us with its presence?
item.description: Earlier this year, a relatively unknown startup from Finland made a startling announcement: It had finally solved solid-state batteries. Not only that, but Donut Lab, a spinoff of Verge...

item.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
item.description: Plus: Iran’s internet blackout hits the 1,000-hour mark, cryptocurrency scams result in a record amount of money stolen from Americans, and more.

item.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
item.description: From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.

item.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
item.description: Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Overfishing in the South Pacific
item.description: Regulation is hard: The South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organization (SPRFMO) oversees fishing across roughly 59 million square kilometers (22 million square miles) of the South...

item.title: Now the FAA says gamers are the answer to its air traffic controller shortage
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has struggled for years to have enough air traffic controllers to address shortages, with the US Government Accountability Office (GAO) saying in January...

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

item.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
item.description: San Francisco police have arrested a 20-year-old man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Russian Hill house early Friday morning, The San Francisco Standard...

item.title: How to watch the Artemis II astronauts return to Earth
item.description: The Orion capsule, carrying Artemis II commander Reid Wiseman, pilot Victor Glover, mission specialist Christina Koch, and Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen, is expected to land back to Earth after...

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: The new AI model is being heralded—and feared—as a hacker’s superweapon. Experts say its arrival is a wake-up call for developers who have long made security an afterthought.

item.title: Microsoft finally lets Windows 11 testers unlock experimental features without ViVeTool
item.description: Microsoft is finally allowing Windows 11 testers to get access to experimental features without having to download a third-party app like ViVeTool. The changes are part of making the Windows...

item.title: My go-to electric screwdriver is on sale for over 50 percent off today
item.description: Fanttik, like Hoto, is quickly becoming a household name. The tool brand’s cordless wares are quiet, capable, and certainly more stylish than anything you’d find at your local Ace Hardware or...

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

item.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
item.description: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. Expect live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on autonomous driving's...

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
item.description: An attacker allegedly threw a molotov cocktail at the OpenAI CEO’s residence before making threats outside of the startup’s headquarters.

item.title: This Startup Wants You to Pay Up to Talk With AI Versions of Human Experts
item.description: Onix is launching a “Substack of bots,” where digital twins of health and wellness influencers dispense advice 24/7. And maybe hawk their products.
